nodes.label: Nicole Brown Simpson
nodes.description: 35-year-old ex-wife of O.J. Simpson. Found murdered outside her Brentwood condo on June 12, 1994.

nodes.label: Ron Goldman
nodes.description: 25-year-old waiter. Found murdered alongside Nicole. Was returning her mother's sunglasses she left at the restaurant.

nodes.label: O.J. Simpson
nodes.description: Former NFL star, actor, and ex-husband of Nicole Brown. Has a history of domestic violence against Nicole.

nodes.label: 875 S. Bundy Drive
nodes.description: Nicole Brown's condo in Brentwood, Los Angeles. The murders took place at the front walkway around 10:15 PM.

nodes.label: 360 N. Rockingham Ave
nodes.description: O.J. Simpson's Brentwood estate, 2 miles from the crime scene. Blood trail found leading to the property.

nodes.label: The Bloody Glove
nodes.description: Leather glove found at Rockingham estate matching one found at Bundy crime scene. Contained blood of both victims and Simpson.

nodes.label: White Bronco Chase
nodes.description: On June 17, 1994, O.J. led police on a slow-speed chase in a white Ford Bronco. He carried a gun, passport, and disguise.

nodes.label: Domestic Violence Records
nodes.description: Nicole called 911 multiple times. In 1989, Simpson pleaded no contest to spousal battery. Photos showed Nicole's bruised face.

nodes.label: DNA Blood Evidence
nodes.description: Blood drops at Bundy matched Simpson. Blood in the Bronco matched both victims. Simpson's blood was at Bundy.

nodes.label: Glove Demonstration — 'If It Doesn't Fit'
nodes.description: In court, Simpson tried on the bloody glove and it appeared too tight. Defense: 'If it doesn't fit, you must acquit.'

nodes.label: Det. Mark Fuhrman
nodes.description: LAPD detective who found the bloody glove at Rockingham. Later exposed for racial slurs, damaging prosecution credibility.

nodes.label: Fuhrman Tapes
nodes.description: Audio recordings of Fuhrman using racial slurs and boasting about planting evidence in other cases. Devastated the prosecution.

nodes.label: Kato Kaelin
nodes.description: House guest living in Simpson's guest house. Heard 3 loud thumps against the wall around 10:40 PM on the night of the murders.

nodes.label: The Missing Hour
nodes.description: Simpson's whereabouts between 9:36 PM and 10:54 PM are unaccounted for. The murders occurred during this window.

nodes.label: Allan Park — Limousine Driver
nodes.description: Arrived at Rockingham at 10:22 PM to take Simpson to the airport. No one answered until 10:54 PM. Saw a large figure enter the house.

nodes.label: Johnnie Cochran
nodes.description: Lead defense attorney. Masterfully introduced racial bias narrative, turning the trial into a referendum on LAPD racism.

nodes.label: O.J. Simpson
nodes.description: O.J. Simpson was a former NFL player and actor who was accused of murdering his ex-wife Nicole Brown Simpson and her friend Ron Goldman. He was found not guilty in a highly publicized trial. Simpson had a history of domestic violence against his ex-wife.

nodes.label: Nicole Brown Simpson
nodes.description: Nicole Brown Simpson was the ex-wife of O.J. Simpson and the mother of their two children. She was brutally murdered outside her condominium in Los Angeles. Nicole had previously reported incidents of domestic violence by O.J. to the police.

nodes.label: Ron Goldman
nodes.description: Ron Goldman was a friend of Nicole Brown Simpson who was also murdered outside her condominium. He was a waiter at a local restaurant and had been returning a pair of glasses to Nicole when they were both killed.

nodes.label: Mark Ferman
nodes.description: The detective who investigated the crime scene and found key pieces of evidence, including the bloody glove. Mark Ferman was a crucial witness in the trial, as he provided testimony about the evidence he found and its significance.

nodes.label: Johnnie Cochran
nodes.description: Johnnie Cochran was a lawyer who was part of O.J. Simpson's defense team. He was known for his charismatic personality and his ability to connect with the jury.

nodes.label: Marcia Clark
nodes.description: Marcia Clark was a prosecutor in the O.J. Simpson trial. She was known for her tough and aggressive questioning of witnesses.

nodes.label: Robert Shapiro
nodes.description: Robert Shapiro was a lawyer who was part of O.J. Simpson's defense team. He was known for his experience in high-profile cases.

nodes.label: Robert Kardashian
nodes.description: Robert Kardashian was a lawyer and a friend of O.J. Simpson who was involved in his defense. He was also the father of the Kardashian siblings.

nodes.label: Lance Ito
nodes.description: Lance Ito was the judge in the O.J. Simpson trial. He was known for his fair and impartial demeanor.

nodes.label: Philip Vader
nodes.description: Philip Vader was a detective with the LAPD who was involved in the investigation of the murders.

nodes.label: Tom Langan
nodes.description: Tom Langan was a detective with the LAPD who was involved in the investigation of the murders.

nodes.label: Brad Roberts
nodes.description: Brad Roberts was a junior partner of Mark Ferman who was involved in the investigation of the murders.

nodes.label: David Rossy
nodes.description: David Rossy was a sergeant with the LAPD who was one of the first officers to arrive at the crime scene.

nodes.label: Marty
nodes.description: Marty was a sergeant with the LAPD who was involved in the investigation of the murders.

nodes.label: Keith Bushley
nodes.description: Keith Bushley was a commander of operations for the Western quarter of LA who was involved in the investigation of the murders.

nodes.label: Henry Lee
nodes.description: Henry Lee was a forensic expert who testified for the defense in the O.J. Simpson trial.

nodes.label: Barry Scheck
nodes.description: Barry Scheck was a lawyer who was part of O.J. Simpson's defense team. He was known for his expertise in DNA evidence.

nodes.label: Peter Neufeld
nodes.description: Peter Neufeld was a lawyer who was part of O.J. Simpson's defense team. He was known for his expertise in DNA evidence.

nodes.label: Robert Kardashian Jr
nodes.description: Robert Kardashian Jr was the son of Robert Kardashian and a friend of O.J. Simpson's children.

nodes.label: Kardashian Siblings
nodes.description: The Kardashian siblings, including Kourtney, Kim, Khloe, and Rob, are the children of Robert Kardashian and were friends of O.J. Simpson's children.

nodes.label: Bloody Glove
nodes.description: A bloody glove found at the crime scene, later matched to another glove found at O.J. Simpson's estate. The glove had bloodstains from both victims and O.J. Simpson. Its discovery was a crucial piece of evidence in the trial.

nodes.label: Bloody Footprints
nodes.description: Bloody footprints found leading away from the crime scene, later matched to O.J. Simpson's shoes. These footprints indicated that the perpetrator was injured and fled the scene. They were a significant piece of evidence linking O.J. Simpson to the crime.

nodes.label: DNA Blood Samples
nodes.description: DNA blood samples collected from the crime scene, O.J. Simpson's estate, and his vehicle. These samples were used to create a DNA profile that matched O.J. Simpson, placing him at the crime scene and linking him to the victims.

nodes.label: Ford Bronco
nodes.description: O.J. Simpson's Ford Bronco, which was the subject of a police chase and later found to have bloodstains from both victims and O.J. Simpson. The Bronco was a key piece of evidence in the trial, as it linked O.J. Simpson to the crime scene and provided a possible means of escape.

nodes.label: Bloodstained Socks
nodes.description: Bloodstained socks found in O.J. Simpson's bedroom, which had blood from both victims on them. These socks were a crucial piece of evidence, as they linked O.J. Simpson to the crime scene and indicated that he had been in contact with the victims' blood.

nodes.label: Knit Hat
nodes.description: A knit hat found at the crime scene, which was later matched to a hat owned by O.J. Simpson. The hat was a significant piece of evidence, as it linked O.J. Simpson to the crime scene and provided a possible means of identifying the perpetrator.

nodes.label: Bloody Envelope
nodes.description: A bloody envelope found at the crime scene, which had O.J. Simpson's fingerprints and blood from one of the victims on it. The envelope was a significant piece of evidence, as it linked O.J. Simpson to the crime scene and provided a possible means of identifying the perpetrator.

nodes.label: Bundy Drive Crime Scene
nodes.description: The location where the bodies of Nicole Brown Simpson and Ron Goldman were found. It is a key crime scene in the O.J. Simpson murder case. The area was thoroughly examined for evidence.

nodes.label: O.J. Simpson's Rockingham Residence
nodes.description: O.J. Simpson's home, where a bloody glove was found. The house was a key location in the investigation, with police finding several pieces of evidence. It was also where O.J. Simpson was arrested.

nodes.label: Mezzaluna Restaurant
nodes.description: The restaurant where Nicole Brown Simpson and Ron Goldman were last seen alive. It is a significant location in the timeline of events leading up to the murders.

nodes.label: LAPD Headquarters
nodes.description: The police station where O.J. Simpson was supposed to turn himself in. It is also where much of the investigation and evidence processing took place.

nodes.label: West LA Police Station
nodes.description: The police station where the two children were taken after the discovery of the bodies. It was also a location where some of the investigation and interviews took place.

nodes.label: Nicole Brown Simpson Murder
nodes.description: June 12, 1994: Nicole Brown Simpson and Ron Goldman were murdered outside Nicole's condominium in Brentwood, California.

nodes.label: OJ Simpson Arrest
nodes.description: June 13, 1994: OJ Simpson failed to turn himself in and became the subject of a police pursuit, which ended in his arrest.

nodes.label: Fredric Rieders Testimony
nodes.description: July 1995: Fredric Rieders testified that the level of EDTA in the evidence samples was higher than that which is normally found in blood.

nodes.label: Glove Trial
nodes.description: June 28, 1995: The infamous glove trial took place, where OJ Simpson tried on the gloves found at the crime scene.

nodes.label: Richard Rubin Testimony
nodes.description: September 12, 1995: Richard Rubin testified that the gloves could have shrunk due to moisture.

nodes.label: OJ Simpson Charged
nodes.description: June 17, 1994: OJ Simpson was formally charged with the murders of Nicole Brown Simpson and Ron Goldman.

nodes.label: Polygraph Test
nodes.description: A polygraph test taken by O.J. Simpson, which reportedly indicated deception. The test results were not admissible in court but may have influenced the defense strategy.

nodes.label: Robert Kardashian's Knowledge of Polygraph Test
nodes.description: Robert Kardashian's awareness of the polygraph test results, which may have affected his perception of O.J. Simpson's guilt.

nodes.label: Ron Ship
nodes.description: A friend of O.J. Simpson and an LAPD officer who had knowledge of O.J.'s violent tendencies. His testimony could have been significant in establishing a motive.

edges.description: O.J. Simpson was married to Nicole Brown Simpson until their divorce in 1992.

Ron Ship knew O.J. Simpson and was aware of his violent tendencies.
Ron Ship also knew Nicole Brown Simpson, which could have given him insight into the dynamics of their relationship.
